intro.description: This cheat sheet guides you through using the Functional Converter cleanup to transform imperative for-each loops into expressive Java 8 Stream pipelines.

item.title: Understand Loop to Stream Conversions
item.description:
Java 8 Streams provide a functional, composable way to process collections. The Functional Converter cleanup identifies loop patterns and rewrites them:
• Simple for-each iteration → collection.forEach(e -> ...)
• Filtering loops → stream().filter(...).forEach(...)
• Mapping/collecting loops → stream().map(...).collect(...)
• Reduction loops → stream().reduce(...)
The cleanup only applies when the transformation is semantically safe (e.g. no early returns that change outer control flow, no checked exceptions).

item.title: Enable the Cleanup
item.description:
Open the Clean Up dialog to enable the Functional Converter cleanup:
1. In the Package Explorer, select a Java project (Java 8 or later)
2. Choose Source → Clean Up…
3. Select Use custom profile and click Configure…
4. On the Functional Converter (Sandbox) tab, enable the desired conversion options
5. Click OK
Click Perform to open the Source menu.

item.title: Run the Cleanup on Java Files
item.description:
Apply the functional conversion cleanup to your Java source files:
1. Open or select Java files containing for-each loops that iterate over collections
2. Choose Source → Clean Up…
3. Confirm the profile has Functional Converter (Sandbox) enabled
4. Click Finish
The cleanup rewrites eligible loops as stream pipelines and adds necessary imports automatically.

item.title: Review the Stream Pipeline Code
item.description:
After the cleanup runs, review the transformed code:
• Simple loops become concise forEach() calls
• Conditional loops include filter() steps
• Loops that build result collections use collect(Collectors.toList()) or similar
• java.util.stream.* imports are added as needed
Verify that stream operations preserve the original semantics by running your test suite after the cleanup. Note that stream pipelines may have different performance characteristics for small collections.
